report_name: TamperedChef Malware: Trojanized Apps Masquerade as Productivity Tools to Deploy Stealthy Payloads

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The primary infection vector is malicious advertising (malvertising) that pushes users toward attacker-controlled websites hosting the trojanized applications.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims are tricked into downloading and running the trojanized productivity application, believing it to be legitimate software.

attack_patterns.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
attack_patterns.description: The TamperedChef application checks for signs of a virtualized or analysis environment before activating its malicious components.

attack_patterns.name: Delay Execution
attack_patterns.description: The malware remains dormant for weeks or months after installation to evade sandbox analysis and behavioral detection systems.

attack_patterns.name: Code Signing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use over 81 unique code signing certificates to make the trojanized software appear legitimate and bypass basic security warnings.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: The malware establishes persistence through common autostart mechanisms like registry run keys to ensure it runs after a system reboot.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Once active, the malware communicates with a C2 server over standard web protocols (HTTP/HTTPS) to download payloads and receive instructions.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The primary function of the malware after activation is to download and execute secondary payloads, such as info-stealers and RATs, from the C2 server.

malware.name: TamperedChef
malware.description: A style of malware involving trojanized productivity applications distributed via malvertising. It remains dormant for extended periods before contacting a C2 server to download and execute secondary payloads like info-stealers, RATs, and proxy tools.

malware.name: AppSuite PDF
malware.description: An example of a TamperedChef-style trojanized application.

malware.name: Calendaromatic
malware.description: An example of a TamperedChef-style trojanized application.

malware.name: JustAskJacky
malware.description: An example of a TamperedChef-style trojanized application.

malware.name: CrystalPDF
malware.description: An example of a TamperedChef-style trojanized application, distributed from crystalpdf.com.

campaigns.name: CL-CRI-1089
campaigns.description: A major cluster of TamperedChef malware activity identified by Unit 42, encompassing a large number of unique samples.

campaigns.name: CL-UNK-1090
campaigns.description: A major cluster of TamperedChef malware activity identified by Unit 42, encompassing a large number of unique samples.

campaigns.name: CL-UNK-1110
campaigns.description: A major cluster of TamperedChef malware activity identified by Unit 42, encompassing a large number of unique samples.

identities.name: Unit 42
identities.description: The security research organization at Palo Alto Networks that identified and analyzed the TamperedChef malware clusters.

infrastructure.name: TamperedChef C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control servers used by TamperedChef malware to download secondary payloads and receive instructions.

infrastructure.name: TamperedChef Distribution Websites
infrastructure.description: Professionally designed websites, such as crystalpdf.com, used to host and distribute trojanized productivity applications.

indicators.name: TamperedChef Distribution Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TamperedChef Distribution Domain DNS Query
id: 934575c8-111b-419b-839f-2930263630f5
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains known to distribute TamperedChef-style malware.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tracking-tampered-chef-clusters/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but could be triggered by security researchers.
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'crystalpdf.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to crystalpdf.com, a domain known for distributing TamperedChef malware.

indicators.name: TamperedChef Trojanized Application Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: TamperedChef Trojanized Application Execution
id: 2b8d0c2e-4e4f-4d9a-9e1a-8c1b2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of suspicious productivity tools that match the naming convention of TamperedChef malware.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tracking-tampered-chef-clusters/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, but unusually named, productivity tools.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|contains:
      - 'pdf-editor'
      - 'zip-converter'
    Image|endswith: '.exe'
  filter:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects execution of executables with names like 'pdf-editor-*.exe' or 'zip-converter-*.exe', which are characteristic of TamperedChef installers.

indicators.name: TamperedChef Delayed Execution via Timeout
indicators.pattern:
title: TamperedChef Delayed Execution via Timeout
id: 5a1b2c3d-4e5f-6a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a command-line pattern used by TamperedChef to delay execution of a payload using the timeout command.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tracking-tampered-chef-clusters/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1678
  - attack.defense_evasion
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate scripts that use timeout for delays.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'timeout '
      - '/t '
      - ' && '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'timeout' command to delay the execution of a subsequent command, a technique used by TamperedChef for evasion.

indicators.name: TamperedChef Persistence via Run Key
indicators.pattern:
title: TamperedChef Persistence via Run Key
id: f0e1d2c3-b4a5-6f7e-8d9c-0b1a2f3e4d5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of registry values in the user's Run key folder, a common persistence technique for TamperedChef.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tracking-tampered-chef-clusters/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installers creating persistence.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'SetValue'
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  filter:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects modification of the HKCU\...\Run registry key, a method used by TamperedChef to persist across reboots.

relationships.target_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
